Japanese: 同日　午後　７時１８分 高菱屋　大展示場
English: October 11, 7:18 PM Lordly Tailor Main Exhibit Hall

Japanese: （“高菱屋（たかびしや）百貨店” 街でイチバンの超高級デパート）
English: ("Lordly Tailor"... The city's fanciest and most expensive department store.)

Japanese: （秘宝展、か。‥‥こいつは あなどれないかもしれないな）
English: (Treasure Exhibit, huh... I have to admit I'm pretty impressed.)

Japanese: うわあ‥‥ 圧倒されちゃうねー！
English: Wow... This is awesome!

Japanese: うーん‥‥どんなガラクタでも、 秘宝っぽく見えるものなんだな。
English: Yeah... You can make even the cheapest junk look great...

Japanese: ‥‥それなりに並べれば。
English: It all depends on how you display it.

Japanese: あ！　お屋敷の物置で、ホコリを かぶっていた掛け軸もありますよ！
English: Oh! They even have the dusty old hanging scroll that was in the Fey Manor storeroom!

Japanese: ああ、あたしが小さいころ、 ラクガキしちゃったヤツね。
English: Oh yeah, I remember scribbling on it when I was a little girl!

Japanese: （たいした秘宝だな‥‥）
English: (On a family heirloom...!?)

Japanese: ね、なるほどくん。
English: Say, Nick...

Japanese: この秘宝展のセキニンシャさんが、 地下の倉庫で待ってるらしいよ。
English: The person in charge of this exhibition is waiting for us in the basement warehouse.

Japanese: ふうん。
English: Alright...

Japanese: （アイサツ、しておいたほうが いいのかな‥‥）
English: (I guess we should go and say hello...)

Japanese: なんだろう、この大げさな台は。 何も置いてないけど。
English: What's with this big cushion? It's just lying around, with nothing on it.

Japanese: ううん‥‥きっと、 スゴいお宝が置かれるんだよ！
English: Hmm... Well it must be for the greatest treasure of all!

Japanese: “スゴいお宝”って‥‥ そんなもの、あったっけ？
English: "Greatest treasure"...? Is there something like that?

Japanese: え！　‥‥そりゃ、あるよねえ はみちゃん！
English: Huh? Why... Of course there is! Right, Pearly?

Japanese: え！　も、もちろんですとも！
English: Oh! Um, yeah! Of course!

Japanese: とにかく、想像も絶するような 秘宝にちがいないね！
English: Yeah, I'll bet it would really take your breath away!

Japanese: この古いビョウブには 見おぼえがあるな。
English: I think I remember this old folding screen here...

Japanese: たしか去年、倉院の里で 起こった殺人事件で、
English: I'm pretty sure it was an important piece of evidence...

Japanese: それとなく重要な 証拠品だったような‥‥。
English: ...in that murder case that happened in Kurain Village.

Japanese: これぞ、わたくしどもの 霊媒道の最高のヒミツ‥‥
English: This folding screen is the greatest of all the spirit channeling secrets...

Japanese: 《倉院の呪詞》全六葉なのです！
English: The Six Leaf Kurain Sacred Writings folding screen!

Japanese: （ヒミツのわりには、イチバン 目立っているけどな）
English: (If it's so secret, why is it the most prominent thing in the exhibit?)

Japanese: まあ！　真宵さま！ これ、見てくださいっ！
English: Oh! Mystic Maya, look! I can't believe it!

Japanese: あ！　これ、倉院の里の ミニチュアだよ、なるほどくん！
English: Ah! It's so cute! It's a little diorama of Kurain Village!

Japanese: ‥‥ううん、たしかに。
English: ...Yeah, look at that.

Japanese: あの、なんとも言えない さみしい感じがよく出てるな。
English: But somehow, it seems to reek of dreariness.

Japanese: これ、真宵さまのおうちですよ！ 《綾里屋敷》って書いてあります！
English: Oh! Mystic Maya! It's your house! It even says, "Fey Manor" on it!

Japanese: きゃっ、ホントだ！
English: Yikes! You're right!

Japanese: ちょっと、アタマのところに 《ウワサの》って書きたしとこう！
English: I kinda want to add "The Famous" in front of it!

Japanese: やめとけよ。‥‥怒られるぞ。
English: Don't do it, Maya. ...They might get mad.

Japanese: 古そうなツボやら何やら。 ‥‥これも秘宝なの？
English: They're just a bunch of old urns... Are these really "treasures", too?

Japanese: そりゃそうだよ！ だって、“秘宝展”だからね！
English: Of course they are! This is a "Treasure Exhibit", isn't it!?

Japanese: ああ。ま、そりゃそうだね。
English: Well yeah, I guess so.

Japanese: （そういうものなのか？ “秘宝展”って‥‥）
English: (So all it takes to be a "treasure" is age? No wonder nobody likes museums...)

Japanese: もめんの布地に、きれいな 文字がビッシリ並んでいる。
English: A piece of cloth with a ton of finely-written characters jammed onto it.

Japanese: 霊媒師の心得みたいなものかな。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Probably esoteric knowledge only mediums would know. ...Hmm, let's see...

Japanese: タイトルは‥‥ “お金のたまる１０８の方法”
English: Here's the title in English... "108 Ways to Save Money".

Japanese: 前に見たときよりも、ちょっと 増えているような気がするな。
English: I'm pretty sure the number has gone up since the last time I saw it.

Japanese: あたしたち、いつだって 研究してるからね！
English: Well, we're always looking for new ways you know!

Japanese: ‥‥霊媒師の生活は、 やはりラクではないみたいだ。
English: Being a medium sounds like a rough way of life...

Japanese: たぶん‥‥倉院のツボが 置かれるはずだったんだろうね。
English: That's probably where the Sacred Urn was going to be displayed.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。
English: Yeah, I guess so...

Japanese: かわりに、ぼくの弁護士バッジ 貸してやろうか？
English: Should I put my attorney's badge there? You know, as a replacement?

Japanese: ううん‥‥ちょっと 小さすぎるんじゃないかな。
English: Hmm... I think it's a little too small for that.

Japanese: あたしの勾玉なんか どうかな。
English: What about my Magatama?

Japanese: ううん‥‥やっぱり 小さすぎるんじゃないかな。
English: Now you're being silly... That's definitely too small.

Japanese: 《倉院の呪詞》が書いてある ビョウブだよ。
English: It's the folding screen of Kurain Sacred Writings.

Japanese: あたしは読めないけどね。
English: But I can't read what it says on it.

Japanese: え！　大切な呪文じゃないの？
English: Really? But I thought it was some kind of important spell!

Japanese: ああ。あたし、おかあさんに 読んでもらっておぼえたから。
English: Ahh, yeah. My mother read it and taught me to memorize it.

Japanese: 忘れたらピンチだね。
English: I'll be in trouble if I ever forget it.

Japanese: （‥‥倉院流霊媒道も ガケっぷちに立たされてるな）
English: (Looks like the Kurain Channeling School is teetering dangerously on the edge...)

Japanese: いやー、よくできてるね。 あたしたちの里のミニチュア。
English: Wow, this is incredible! It's a tiny version of my whole village!

Japanese: あ、そうだ！　なるほどくんも、 事務所のミニチュアを作ろうよ！
English: Hey! I've got a great idea! We can make a miniature of your office, Nick!

Japanese: 事務所、どうでもいいガラクタが あふれかえっているからな。
English: I don't know about that. The whole place is overflowing with junk.

Japanese: カンゼンに再現しようとしたら、 死ぬまでかかっても終わらないよ。
English: You'd die before you finish painstakingly recreating that mess.

Japanese: その前に、お部屋を 片づければいいじゃない。
English: Well, we could always clean it up first.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: *shrug* True.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 高菱屋　大展示場
English: October 12 Lordly Tailor Main Exhibition Hall

Japanese: さあ、探しましょう！ ワルモノの手がかりを！
English: Right! Let's see if we can find some clues that might lead us to the bad guy!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、パッと見た感じ、 荒らされた感じはしないぞ。
English: ...But it doesn't look like anything has been disturbed.

Japanese: そうだねえ。 ゆうべ見たまんまだよ。
English: Yeah, it looks pretty much the same as it did last night.

Japanese: ううう‥‥ワルモノめぇ‥‥
English: Hmmm... Bad guy, huh.

Japanese: コラッ！　アンタたち！ 入ってきちゃダメッス！
English: Hey! You can't poke around here, pal!

Japanese: （‥‥やれやれ、この声。 まさか、また‥‥）
English: Wait a minute... that voice. Oh no! Not him again!

Japanese: まァた、アンタたちッスか！ “やれやれ”は、こっちッス！
English: It's YOU!! And I'm the one who should be saying "not again"!

Japanese: 事件が起こると、なんで かならず出てくるッス？
English: Why is it that every time something bad happens, you always show up, pal?

Japanese: それは、こっちが聞きたいですよ。 他の刑事さん、いないんですか？
English: I should be asking you that. Aren't there any other detectives?

Japanese: それは、こっちが聞きたいッス。 なんで、現場をウロウロするッス？
English: No, I should be asking YOU that. What are you doing hanging around here?

Japanese: それは、こっちが聞きたいですよ。 あなたこそ、どうして‥‥
English: Hey, I asked you first. Why are you here any--

Japanese: ま。ま。仲よくやりましょうよ。
English: Alright, alright, we got it! Why don't you two kiss and make up already?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。おひさしぶりです！ おひげの刑事さん。
English: ...Um, hello. It's been a long time, Mr. Scruffy Detective!

Japanese: お！　おじょうちゃんッスか。 ‥‥ええと‥‥ッス‥‥
English: Oh! It's you, little missy! ...Um, ahh...

Japanese: おニイさんの名前は糸鋸　圭介。 “イトノコギリケイスケ”ッス！
English: Actually, my name is Gumshoe. "Detective Dick Gumshoe"!

Japanese: このチャンスに、バッチリ おぼえてほしいッス！
English: Now is a good chance for you to try to remember it right!

Japanese: 長ったらしいからね。 “イトノコ刑事”でいいよ。
English: And if it's too long for you, you can just call him "Dick".

Japanese: はいっ！　いとのこ刑事さん。 よろしくおねがいします！
English: Okay! It's good to see you again, Mr. Detective Dick!

Japanese: よ‥‥よろしくおねがいするッス。
English: Um... Yeah... Good to see you again, too, I guess.

Japanese: あの。いとのこ刑事さん！ 事件のこと、教えてください！
English: Umm, Mr. Detective Dick! Can you tell us about what happened?

Japanese: お。ベンキョウ熱心ッスね。 感心カンシン。
English: Oh, you're trying to remember my name! Atta girl! ...Um, just... It's "Gumshoe", OK?

Japanese: ええと、盗まれたのは 《倉院のツボ》と言って‥‥
English: Anyway, the thief stole something called the "Sacred Urn"...

Japanese: 知ってます。
English: I know about that.

Japanese: あ。そ、それで、 犯人の名前は《仮面マスク》‥‥
English: Oh. W-Well, the criminal's name is Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: それも知ってます。
English: I know about that, too.

Japanese: イトノコさん、もうちょっと マシな情報をください！
English: Detective, can't you tell us something other than that?

Japanese: ‥‥そッスね‥‥。
English: W-Well...

Japanese: アンタたちには、世話になった ような気がしないでもないし。
English: I guess I sorta kinda owe you guys... in a way...

Japanese: 事件が起こったのは昨夜、 夜中の１時３０分のコトッス。
English: The crime occurred last night at around 1:30 in the morning.

Japanese: ずいぶん、ハッキリ わかってるんですね。
English: How exactly do you know that?

Japanese: 犯行現場で見張っていた男から、 非常電話が入ったッス。
English: We got an emergency phone call from a guard at the scene of the crime, pal.

Japanese: “たった今、ツボが盗まれた！” ‥‥って。
English: He said, "The urn was just stolen!"

Japanese: 犯行現場‥‥って、 ここじゃないんですか？
English: The scene of the crime... You mean here, right?

Japanese: ちがうッス。
English: Wrong.

Japanese: あのツボは、地下倉庫に 厳重に保管されていたッス。
English: That urn was being kept under careful guard in the basement warehouse.

Japanese: （地下倉庫、か‥‥）
English: (Basement warehouse, huh...)

Japanese: あの。ちかそうこを見張っていた 殿方というのは‥‥？
English: Umm, so who was guarding the basement warehouse...?

Japanese: マッタク！　怪人を取り逃がした のは、アイツのせいッス。
English: That guy! It's all his fault that the thief got away, pal!

Japanese: たった１回、手がらを立てて 調子に乗った、あの名探偵の‥‥！
English: He solves one case and gets a swelled head, thinking that he's an Ace Detective!

Japanese: め、メイタンテー‥‥？
English: A-Ace Detective...?

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。その‥‥ 《仮面マスク》ですけど。
English: Hey, Detective Gumshoe, about this Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: 有名なんですか？
English: Is he famous?

Japanese: うええええええっ！ ナニ言ってるの、なるほどくん！
English: Whaaaat!? You're joking, right Nick?

Japanese: 仮面マスクといえば、今、 サイコーにホットな怪人じゃない！
English: Mask☆DeMasque is the hottest thing since sliced bread!

Japanese: あるときは博物館の警備員に 化けた仮面マスク！
English: Sometimes he appears as a museum guard!

Japanese: あるときは美術館の受付嬢に 化けた仮面マスク！
English: Sometimes he appears as a big brown bear known to surprise unsuspecting victims.

Japanese: そして、その正体はッ！ ‥‥スガオの仮面マスクッス。
English: But underneath those disguises is... the true Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ‥‥ケッキョク、いつも 仮面マスクじゃないですか。
English: ...So he's some kind of master of disguise, huh.

Japanese: 高価な美術品ばかりを狙い、 ゆうべが５回目の犯行ッス！
English: And he only goes after the finest works of art, pal. Last night was his 5th heist!

Japanese: 高菱屋が協力してくれていれば、 今度こそタイホできていたのに‥‥
English: If Lordly Tailor had only cooperated, I could have caught him this time...

Japanese: え！　知ってたんですか？ 倉院のツボが狙われていたコト！
English: What!? Then you knew he was planning on stealing the Sacred Urn!?

Japanese: そりゃそッス！
English: Of course I did!

Japanese: ヤツめ、今回も予告状を たたきつけてきたッスからねえ。
English: The guy always sends his calling card before he steals something.

Japanese: （予告状‥‥か。 テレビでも言ってたな‥‥）
English: (Calling card, huh... They mentioned that on TV...)

Japanese: いいトシして“名探偵”は ないでしょう。
English: So why do you call him an "Ace Detective" anyway?

Japanese: しかたないッス。 本人がそう言ってるッスから。
English: That's what he calls himself, pal... I didn't make it up.

Japanese: どんなヒトなんですか？
English: So? What's he like?

Japanese: 仮面マスクは、これまで５回、 犯行をかさねているッス。
English: Well, like I told you, Mask☆DeMasque has made five heists so far.

Japanese: あの探偵は、４回目の事件で、 怪人からエモノを取り戻したッス。
English: On the 4th heist, Mr. Ace Detective managed to get the stolen treasure back.

Japanese: まあ、すばらしい！ たったヒトリで！
English: Wow, that is impressive! All by himself!?

Japanese: ホント、フシギなオトコッス。
English: Yeah. I gotta admit it, pal. Maybe he really is as good as he says.

Japanese: ワレワレはいつも、予告のあった 現場に張りこんでいるッスが‥‥
English: Every time the thief sends one of his calling cards, we send our men on a stakeout...

Japanese: ヤツの姿を見かけたことが ないッス。それなのに‥‥
English: But none of us has ever gotten so much as a glimpse of the guy...

Japanese: どうやって、かいじんさんから エモノを取り戻したのでしょう‥‥
English: But Mr. Ace Detective was able to retrieve the stolen treasure from the thief...

Japanese: 今も、たったヒトリで犯行現場に はいつくばって、調べてるッス。
English: Anyway, he's at the scene of the crime right now, looking for clues.

Japanese: （犯行現場‥‥地下倉庫、か）
English: (So he's down in the basement warehouse, huh...?)

Japanese: “予告状”というのは？
English: So what's this "calling card"?

Japanese: ダレにも見せちゃダメッスよ。 ‥‥これッス。
English: Here it is. But don't show it to anyone! It's top-secret, OK, pal?

Japanese: このマークは‥‥？
English: What's this mark on the front?

Japanese: 仮面マスクのエンブレムッス。
English: That's Mask☆DeMasque's signature emblem, pal. You could say it's his "mark".

Japanese: ヤツの予告状には、コイツが かならずプリントされてるッス。
English: All of his calling cards have got it on them.

Japanese: テレビじゃ言ってませんでしたね。
English: They didn't mention THAT on the news flash.

Japanese: 世間には公表していないッス。
English: We're withholding that detail from the public for "operational reasons"...

Japanese: 予告状がホンモノかどうか、 このエンブレムでわかるッス。
English: The only way we can tell if a calling card is real or not is by this emblem.

Japanese: 証拠品《予告状》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Calling Card added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。有名になれば、 ニセモノも出るからねー。
English: ...That makes sense. When you get famous, there are always imitators.

Japanese: なるほどくんも、ニセモノ出現を めざしてがんばりましょうよ！
English: Mr. Nick, if you work a little harder, maybe you can have your own copycats someday!

Japanese: う、うん。がんばるよ。
English: Um, uh, thanks... I guess.

Japanese: ほら。弁護士のバッジです。
English: Here, take a look at my attorney's badge.

Japanese: まだ、そんなちっぽけな バッジにこだわってるッスか！
English: Are you still showing that cheap little thing off?

Japanese: やっぱり男は、 ダマって警察手帳ッス。
English: A real man keeps his mouth shut... and carries a suave police badge, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥じつはちょっぴり、 ミョーな気もするッス。
English: ...To be honest, pal, there's something that bugs me.

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What's that?

Japanese: これまで盗まれたモノは、 自分でも聞いたことがあるような、
English: All the other items that were stolen by Mask☆DeMasque were ones even I had heard of.

Japanese: 名のある美術品ばかり だったッス。
English: They were all world-famous treasures.

Japanese: 《エマノンの涙》とか、 《ボンゴラの宝冠》とか‥‥
English: The "Tear of Emanon" or the "Crown of Bongora"...

Japanese: 《倉院のツボ》だって有名ですよ！ ‥‥ね！　はみちゃん！
English: The "Sacred Urn of Kurain" is famous, too! ...Right, Pearly!?

Japanese: ええ！　なにしろ、 わたくしも知っていますから！
English: Of course! Everyone in Kurain Village knows about it!

Japanese: （どうやら、気まぐれな 怪人クンらしいな‥‥）
English: (Sounds like we've got an unpredictable thief on our hands...)

Japanese: いッスか！　言っちゃダメッスよ。 エンブレムのコト。
English: Remember! Don't tell anyone about that emblem. OK, pal?

Japanese: この予告状‥‥警察には 届かないんですか？
English: Does the thief ever send these to the police?

Japanese: ザンネンながら、いつも被害者の ところだけッス。
English: No, always to the victims.

Japanese: これまでの被害者は、警察に 助けを求めてきたッスが‥‥
English: Up until now, the victims have always come to us looking for help though.

Japanese: ‥‥今回は、 そうじゃなかったんですか？
English: ...That's not what happened this time, you mean?

Japanese: ま、そッスな。
English: You got that right, pal.

Japanese: いッスか！　もしアンタたちが 怪人の予告状を受け取ったら‥‥
English: Listen up! If you guys ever get one of these calling cards...

Japanese: ちゃんと、ワレワレに 届けるッスよ！
English: ...I want you to come to us right away! Got me?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: ...Yeah, sure.

Japanese: あの‥‥これなんですけど。
English: Umm, what about this...?

Japanese: ‥‥すまねッス。
English: ...Sorry, pal.

Japanese: ゆうべ、あんまり寝てないッスから アタマがはたらかないッス。
English: I didn't get much sleep last night and my brain's not really working right.

Japanese: ぐっすり寝たら、アタマが はたらくみたいな言い方だね。
English: Somehow I don't think sleep would really fix the problem.

Japanese: やめとけよ。
English: Hey, keep it down.

Japanese: 耳が悪いからいいものの、 もし聞こえてたら怒られるぞ。
English: He might have really good hearing for all you know, and might get mad if he heard.

Japanese: あの‥‥ ゼンブ聞こえてるッス。
English: Ummm... I can hear everything you are saying, pal.

Japanese: サエないカオッスな。
English: Pretty goofy-looking face, if you ask me.
Hey...!

Japanese: 持ち歩かないほうが いいんじゃないかな、それ。
English: What are you doing carrying that around with you anyway? It's not like we ever need it.

Japanese: （ホントだな‥‥）
English: (She has got a point...)

Japanese: 自分は、ヤツの最初の犯行から コイツを追っかけてるッス。
English: I've been on this guy's tail ever since his first heist, pal!

Japanese: 負けつづけ、ってワケですか。
English: Yeah, you're doing a really great job, I can see.

Japanese: ‥‥ぐむむゥ。
English: ...Oof!

Japanese: 高くジャンプするためには、 低くしゃがまなけりゃならねッス！
English: Listen pal, if you wanna jump high, first you gotta crouch down, right?

Japanese: 今日の、このクヤシサこそが！ いつか、勝利へのカギになるッス！
English: I'm a little frustrated now, but it'll make it that much sweeter when I catch him!

Japanese: ‥‥と、自分に 言い聞かせる毎日ッス。
English: ...Anyway, that's what I keep telling myself every day.

Japanese: がんばってください！ いとのこ刑事さん！
English: Well good luck, Mr. Detective Dick! I believe in you!

Japanese: そう言ってくれるのは おじょうちゃんだけッス！
English: Aww, ain't you sweet. You're the only one that's ever said that!

Japanese: （‥‥キノドクに、けっこう マイってるみたいだな）
English: (...The only one? That's kinda sad if you think about it.)

Japanese: あ。‥‥綾里　千尋さんッスな。 キレイなヒト、だったッス。
English: Oh! That's Ms. Mia Fey! She was a truly beautiful person, pal.

Japanese: え‥‥あの。知ってるんですか？ お姉ちゃんのコト‥‥
English: Huh? You knew her? You knew my sis?

Japanese: 警察の関係者で、彼女のことを 知らない人間はいないッス。
English: Sure. Everybody in the police force knew her.

Japanese: ‥‥惜しいヒトをなくしたッス。
English: ...We all lost a great friend that day...

Japanese: いとのこ刑事さん‥‥
English: Mr. Scruffy Detective...

Japanese: な、泣いてなんかいねッス！ ギンバエが目に入っただけッス！
English: I-I just got something in my eye...! ...I'm not crying!

Japanese: あの‥‥ このヒトなんですけど‥‥
English: Umm... What about this person...?

Japanese: ‥‥すまねッス。
English: ...Sorry, pal.

Japanese: ゆうべ、あんまり寝てないッスから アタマがはたらかないッス。
English: I didn't get much sleep last night and my brain's not really working right.

Japanese: ぐっすり寝たら、アタマが はたらくみたいな言い方だね。
English: Somehow I don't think sleep would really fix the problem.

Japanese: やめとけよ。
English: Hey, keep it down.

Japanese: 耳が悪いからいいものの、 もし聞こえてたら怒られるぞ。
English: He might have really good hearing for all you know, and might get mad if he heard.

Japanese: あの‥‥ ゼンブ聞こえてるッス。
English: Ummm... I can hear everything you are saying, pal.

Japanese: お。なるほどくん。 携帯電話が鳴ってるよ！
English: Oh, Nick! Your cell phone's ringing!

Japanese: もしもし、成歩堂ですけど‥‥
English: Hello, Phoenix Wright here.

Japanese: うわーっはっはっはっはっはっはっ はっはっはっはっはっはっはっはっ
English: Oho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: イトノコギリッス。
English: It's me. Gumshoe.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事！ ど、どうしたんですか？
English: Gumshoe! What is it? What's so funny!?

Japanese: ついに‥‥ついに、 あのクソ探偵に勝ったッスゥ！
English: I'll tell you what's funny, pal! I finally beat him! I beat Mr. Ace Detective!

Japanese: ついに‥‥ついに、 自分は‥‥クソ探偵にィィィィ！
English: Finally! This underdog is the alpha dog today! That stuck-up detective's met his match!

Japanese: やったッス！　快挙ッス！ とッつかまえたッス！
English: Yup! I finally caught him! Lock, stock and barrel! There's no escape for him!

Japanese: “とっつかまえた”って、 もしかして‥‥
English: You "caught him"? ...Are you saying...?

Japanese: 仮面マスクを タイホしたんですか！
English: You... You captured Mask☆DeMasque!?

Japanese: そッスゥゥゥッ！
English: Bingoooo!

Japanese: ‥‥ま。よりセイカクに言えば、 自首してきたッスが。
English: Well... Actually, he surrendered... ...But I still got him!

Japanese: （仮面マスクが‥‥自首？）
English: (Mask☆DeMasque surrendered?)

Japanese: それで、いとのこ刑事さん！ 倉院のツボは‥‥！
English: That's great! So when can we come and pick up the Sacred Urn?

Japanese: あ、ああ‥‥ そのコトなんスけど‥‥
English: Um, ahh, well ya see... I was gonna tell you...
What...? Something wrong?

Japanese: ちょっと、アンタたち、 留置所まで来てもらえねッスか？
English: Do you think you can swing by the detention center sometime?

Japanese: 留置所‥‥？
English: Detention center...?

Japanese: ハナシがあるみたいッス。 ‥‥仮面マスクが、アンタに。
English: He wants to talk to you... Mask☆DeMasque, that is.

Japanese: ええええええええッ！ 仮面マスクが‥‥なるほどくんに？
English: Whaaaat!? He wants to talk to Nick?

Japanese: じゃ、待ってるッス！
English: I'll be waiting for you, pal. So get over here soon.

Japanese: （留置所、ねえ‥‥）
English: (The detention center, huh?)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 高菱屋　大展示場
English: October 12 Lordly Tailor Main Exhibition Hall

Japanese: ま‥‥真宵さんッ！
English: M-Maya...!

Japanese: あ、霧緒さん‥‥
English: Oh, Ms. Andrews...

Japanese: ごめんなさい、私のせいで！ だ‥‥大事なツボが！
English: I'm so sorry! It's my fault! ...Your precious urn!

Japanese: 秘宝が‥‥ ひほうがあああああああッ！
English: Your... precious... UUUUURNNN!!

Japanese: おおお、落ちついてください！
English: Please! C-Calm down! What's wrong?

Japanese: ううう‥‥やっぱり私、 ナニやってもシッパイばっかり。
English: *sniffle* It never ends! Everything I touch ends in failure!

Japanese: 真宵さん、ごめんなさい！ 私‥‥もう、なんでもしますから！
English: Maya, I'm sorry...! I'll do anything to make it up to you!

Japanese: い、いえ。そんなに気にしな
English: N-No, it's okay, don--

Japanese: こう見えても手先は器用ですから、 なんでしたら、かわりのツボを‥‥
English: I know I don't look it, but I'm good with my hands. I could make you another urn.

Japanese: ま、まあまあ。とにかく、 お話を聞かせてください。
English: Hold on, just wait, OK? Breathe, calm down and talk to us.

Japanese: ごめんなさい、ごめんなさい‥‥
English: F-Forgive me! Forgive me!

Japanese: あの‥‥仮面マスクから 予告状が届いたのは‥‥？
English: Umm... So when did you get the calling card from Mask☆DeMasque?

Japanese: あれは‥‥今から１０日前でした。
English: Let's see... Exactly 10 days ago.

Japanese: 警察に届けようと思ったんですけど ‥‥探偵さんに止められて。
English: I was going to show it to the police... But that detective stopped me.

Japanese: あの。哀牙探偵に 警備を依頼したんですね？
English: Um, so you asked Detective Atmey to help with security?

Japanese: はい。おねがいしたのは‥‥ もう、２０日ほど前になります。
English: Yes. In fact, it was about 20 days ago that I hired him.

Japanese: あの方、警察よりも 怪人に詳しいということでした。
English: He seemed to know much more about Mask☆DeMasque than the police.

Japanese: 予告状が届く前から 警備を依頼したんですか。
English: So you hired him for security even before the calling card arrived?

Japanese: なんだか、イヤな予感が したものですから‥‥。
English: Well, yes. I had a premonition that something bad might happen...

Japanese: 私、イヤな予感だけは 自信があるんです！
English: I've learned to trust my hunches!

Japanese: 地下倉庫の防犯カメラや コンピュータは、どなたが‥‥？
English: So that's why there are security cameras, even in the basement warehouse?

Japanese: 防犯用機材は、高菱屋に 用意していただきました。
English: Yes. Lordly Tailor is very serious about their security measures.

Japanese: “怪人め、いつでも来い！” って思っていたら‥‥
English: It was their way of saying, "Bring it on!" to any potential thieves...

Japanese: （ゆうべ、来たワケか‥‥）
English: (Well, he sure "brought it" last night... And even left with a nice souvenir.)

Japanese: 秘宝展の警備について 聞かせてください。
English: Um, can you tell us a bit about the security for the Treasure Exhibit?

Japanese: 私がワルいんですッ！
English: It was all my fault!

Japanese: 大したモノもないのに《秘宝展》 なんて名前をつけちゃったから‥‥
English: I never should have called this paltry little collection a "treasure exhibit" anyway...

Japanese: でも‥‥盗まれた倉院のツボは、 かなりダイジな秘宝だとか‥‥
English: Why do you say that? The urn that was stolen is a pretty important object...

Japanese: ええ。ただ‥‥鑑定の結果、 金銭的な価値は、ほぼゼロです。
English: Maybe, but it's actual value after appraisal was... well, practically zero.

Japanese: ぜろ‥‥
English: Zero!?

Japanese: 私、いっしょうけんめい ピカピカにみがいて、
English: I polished it until it was just about glowing...

Japanese: 少しでも高そうに見えるように がんばったんですよ！
English: I thought maybe I could make it look more valuable!

Japanese: 供子さまのタマシイ入りなのに‥‥
English: B-But that urn contains the soul of Mystic Ami...

Japanese: とにかく、警備はすべて 哀牙さんにお任せしました。
English: Anyway, I left all the security arrangements to Detective Atmey.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: ５日前から、地方からの展示品が どんどん届きだしたんです。
English: Five days ago, I began receiving all sorts of other exhibit items from Kurain.

Japanese: だから‥‥いろいろなヒトが 倉庫に出入りしました。
English: Lots of people started going in and out of the warehouse down there.

Japanese: じゃあ、その中に 仮面マスクがいたかも‥‥！
English: So maybe one of them was actually Mask☆DeMasque in disguise...!

Japanese: いえ。倉庫に出入りした人物は、 私もチェックしましたから。
English: No, I personally checked out everyone that came through here, so that's not possible.

Japanese: （霧緒さんのコトだ。 シッカリやっているだろうな）
English: (Knowing Adrian, she probably even checked out what they ate for breakfast that morning.)

Japanese: あの。‥‥霧緒さん？
English: Umm... Ms. Andrews?

Japanese: わたしがッ！ わたしがゼンブ、悪いんです！
English: I know, I know! It's all my fault!

Japanese: かくなる上は、私があたらしい 倉院のツボを作って‥‥
English: But what's done is done... I'll make it up by making a new urn for you...

Japanese: （ダメだ‥‥何を見せても ムダっぽいな‥‥）
English: (I'm just wasting my time. She's too far gone...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 高菱屋　地下倉庫
English: October 11 Lordly Tailor Basement Warehouse

Japanese: （さすが“地下倉庫”と 言うだけあって‥‥）
English: (Yup. This is a basement warehouse alright...)

Japanese: おば、おばけでも 出そうですね‥‥。
English: Th-This place is scary... I feel like a monster could jump out at any time...

Japanese: だいじょうぶ。あたしが 守ってあげるからね、はみちゃん！
English: Don't be scared, Pearly! I'll protect you!

Japanese: ‥‥あら‥‥、成歩堂さん。
English: ...Oh... ...Mr. Wright.

Japanese: きゃあああっ、出た！ なるほどくんのおばけだっ！
English: Eeeeeeeek!! A monster! You were right!

Japanese: （なんで、ぼくなんだよ‥‥）
English: (Huh? A monster...?)

Japanese: こんばんは。 ‥‥おひさしぶりです。
English: Good evening. ...It's good to see you again.

Japanese: あ‥‥あ。あなたは‥‥
English: Y-You... I know you...

Japanese: ふふ。おじょうちゃんも、 ひさしぶりね。
English: Ha ha. Nice to see you again too, little miss.

Japanese: あ。こっ、こんばんはっ！
English: Ah! H-Hello!

Japanese: なるほどくん、知り合いなの？ それに、はみちゃんも‥‥
English: Is she a friend of yours, Nick? ...And how do you know her, Pearly?

Japanese: はじめまして。 華宮　霧緒（かみやきりお）です。
English: Nice to meet you. I'm Adrian Andrews.

Japanese: 《秘宝展》のプロモーションと 企画を担当していますの。
English: I'm in charge of the promotion and planning for this Treasure Exhibit.

Japanese: 警備システムの手配も やらせていただいています。
English: I'm also responsible for the security arrangements.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥。 よ、よろしくおねがいします。
English: Oh, um... N-Nice to meet you.

Japanese: （霧緒さん‥‥なんか、 フンイキが変わったな‥‥）
English: (Adrian sure has changed since we last met...)

Japanese: 値打ちのある展示品は、まだ ここにしまってあるんですよ。
English: So this is the warehouse. The really valuable exhibit items are still stored away in here.

Japanese: ねえ、真宵さま！ あの扉の横、見てください！
English: Mystic Maya! Take a look next to the door!

Japanese: あれって‥‥供子（きょうこ）さま ですよね！
English: That... That's Mystic Ami, right?

Japanese: そうよ。倉院流霊媒道の創始者・ 綾里　供子の黄金像。
English: Yes, that's a statue of the woman who invented the Kurain Channeling Technique, Ami Fey.

Japanese: 倉院流・分家の霊媒師さんの 霊場からお借りしてきたの。
English: This statue is on loan from one of the training halls of the Fey branch family.

Japanese: 今日のおひるに、ここへ 届いたばかりなんですよ。
English: In fact, it just arrived this morning.

Japanese: 《綾里　供子の黄金像》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Ami Fey's Golden Statue added to the Court Record.

Japanese: あの、手に持っている ブッソウなモノ、なんなの？
English: What's that creepy-looking thing she's holding?

Japanese: あ、あたしに聞かないでよ！
English: Don't ask me! How should I know...?

Japanese: ‥‥だって、 家元のムスメだろ？
English: ...Well, you ARE the future Master, right?

Japanese: あんな黄金像、あたしも 見るの、初めてだもん。
English: Maybe so, but this is the first time I've ever seen this statue.

Japanese: 《倉院の七支刀（しちしとう）》。 儀式で使われていたようです。
English: That "thing" is the Kurain Shichishito. It's a ceremonial sword.

Japanese: 武器ではありませんから、 刃は研いでありませんけど。
English: It's not a real weapon, so the blade isn't sharp.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ、つまんないの。 何か切ってみたかったのに。
English: ...Aww, phooey. I wanted to cut something!

Japanese: ぼくを見つめて言うなよ。
English: Why are you eyeing me for!?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 あの大きな扉はなんですか？
English: Mr. Nick, what's that big door over there?

Japanese: 扉の向こうが倉庫に なっているみたいだね。
English: It looks like it's the door to the actual storeroom.

Japanese: いろんな大きさのハコが こっちから見えるよ。
English: I can see lots of big boxes and stuff back there.

Japanese: え！　おもしろそう！ 行ってみようよ！
English: Really? Let's go in! I wanna take a look!

Japanese: あ‥‥すみません！
English: Oh... I'm sorry!

Japanese: その扉の向こうは、 立入禁止なんですよ。
English: Unauthorized personnel aren't allowed in there.

Japanese: ‥‥立入禁止なんだよ、 なるほどくん。
English: ...Did you hear that, Nick? We're unauthorized personnel.

Japanese: なんでぼくに言うんだよ‥‥
English: Why are you telling me that?

Japanese: 防犯カメラだ。 倉庫の入り口をとらえている。
English: It's a security camera. It's trained on the warehouse door.

Japanese: あの扉を出入りすると、 写真を撮るようになっています。
English: It's set up to take a picture of anyone that enters or exits the door.

Japanese: なにごとも、 用心がダイジですからね。
English: An ounce of prevention is worth a pound of cure, they say.

Japanese: ホント、ひさしぶりですね 成歩堂さん。
English: It really has been a long time, hasn't it, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: なるほどくん、どーいうコト！ この女のヒト、だれよ！
English: Nick! What's going on!? Who is this woman!?

Japanese: なな、なんだよそのノリは。
English: Wh-Why are you spazzing out on me!?

Japanese: なーんて。
English: What?

Japanese: いやあ、このほうが 盛り上がるかな、って。
English: I just thought it'd be more dramatic if I got all worked up. That's all.

Japanese: 真宵さま！ もっと言っておやりなさい！
English: Mystic Maya! You shouldn't let him off the hook so easily!

Japanese: （この子をシゲキするようなコト、 言わないでほしいな‥‥）
English: (Please, Maya, don't say anything that'll needle Pearls further...)

Japanese: 成歩堂さんには、それは お世話になったんですよ。
English: Mr. Wright was there for me when I really needed help.

Japanese: あの‥‥半年前の事件で。
English: It... It was something that happened 7 months ago.

Japanese: ほら。例の 《トノサマン・丙！》の事件。
English: You remember, don't you? The "Nickel Samurai" case.

Japanese: あ、アレかあ‥‥。あたし、 よく知らないんだよねー。
English: Not really... I was stuck in a dark wine cellar... Did that escape your memory, Nick?

Japanese: あれから私、マネージャーを やめて、今のシゴトに。
English: After that, I quit being a manager and started this job.

Japanese: タイヘンでしたね。 ぼくたちのせいで‥‥
English: Wow, that's tough. I'm sorry, Ms. Andrews. If it wasn't for us...

Japanese: とんでもない！ 悪かったのは、私ですから。
English: No! Don't say that! I'm glad the whole thing happened.

Japanese: 成歩堂さんたちのおかげで‥‥ 私、変わることができたんです。
English: Thanks to you and everyone else that was involved, I was able to change my whole life.

Japanese: ‥‥本当に、感謝しています。
English: ...I really am grateful.

Japanese: （よかったな‥‥ そう言ってもらえると）
English: (Whew, that's a relief...)

Japanese: でも、スゴいですね、霧緒さん。
English: I'm really impressed, Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: こんな大きなイベントを 仕切ってるなんて‥‥。
English: Handling a huge exhibition like this must be very tiring.

Japanese: 高菱屋といえば、創業２００年を むかえる、伝統あるデパートです。
English: Well, Lordly Tailor is celebrating their 200th anniversary this year.

Japanese: 大展示場で開かれる催し物も、 超一流のものばかりなんですよ。
English: The works on display in the main exhibition hall are worthy of the finest museums.

Japanese: やったよ、はみちゃん！ イチリューだよ、あたしたち。
English: Woohoo! Did you hear that, Pearly!? The finest museums!

Japanese: ええ、ええ、それはもう。 ホントによかったですねえ。
English: Yes, I did! That's really wonderful.

Japanese: この地下倉庫のセキュリティ‥‥ ずいぶん大げさですね？
English: So this basement warehouse is pretty well-protected, huh?

Japanese: え、ええ‥‥。 だってホラ、秘宝展ですから。
English: ...Of course it is. After all, there are some priceless treasures here.

Japanese: まあ、警備のほうも厳重に‥‥
English: The security guards are all very highly trained as well...

Japanese: （なんだろう‥‥。霧緒さん、 浮かないカオをしてるな‥‥）
English: (That sounds good... So, what is with that face she's making...?)

Japanese: そのバッジのおかげ‥‥ なんですよね。
English: I owe a great deal of thanks to that badge...

Japanese: ‥‥今、私がこうして がんばっていられるのも。
English: ...It's the only reason that I'm still here today.

Japanese: いや‥‥ちがいますよ。
English: Oh, come on now. That's not true...

Japanese: 『バッジじゃなくて、 このボクのおかげなんですよ！』
English: I think what Nick means to say is, "It wasn't thanks to the badge, it was thanks to ME!"

Japanese: ‥‥と、こう言いたいワケだよね、 なるほどくんとしては。
English: Tell the truth, Nick! I know that's what you were thinking!

Japanese: もっとちがうよ！
English: N-No way, Maya!

Japanese: 私、成歩堂さんたちに、 何かお礼をしたいと思ったんです。
English: I wanted to do something nice for you and your friends, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: それで、この秘宝展を‥‥？
English: So that's why you arranged this exhibit...?

Japanese: ええ。成歩堂さんの助手の 真宵さんのふるさとを、
English: I knew that Kurain Village was the hometown of your assistant, Maya.

Japanese: 世間のみなさんに、広く 知っていただければ‥‥と思って。
English: I thought it would be great to let the rest of the world know about it, too.

Japanese: （それはベツに、ぼくに対する お礼ではないような‥‥）
English: Hmm... But that's not exactly thanking me, per se, is it?

Japanese: だって《成歩堂家・秘宝展》なんか だれも見に来ないでしょ。
English: Maybe, but who'd wanna see an exhibit on the "Treasures of Phoenix Wright"?

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃそうだな。
English: ...I guess I can't argue with that one.

Japanese: あの‥‥これなんですけど。
English: Umm... So what about this?

Japanese: すみません。 私、特に気づいたことは‥‥
English: Sorry I can't be of more help, but I really don't have anything to say about that.

Japanese: 半年前の事件で、やっと ふんぎりがついたんです。
English: I've finally managed to put the events of 7 months ago behind me.

Japanese: 今のシゴト、とっても 気に入ってるし‥‥
English: I really love the work that I'm doing now...

Japanese: ホント、成歩堂さんの おかげですよね。
English: Honestly, I owe it all to you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: そんなことないですよ！
English: Oh, it was nothing.

Japanese: あと、おじょうちゃんもね。
English: And to this little girl, too.

Japanese: あ、ありがとうございます！
English: Th-Thank you for saying so!

Japanese: ‥‥どうせ、あたしの 居場所なんて‥‥
English: ...I guess I wasn't much of a help, huh?

Japanese: ごご、ごめんなさい！ みなさんのおかげです！
English: N-No, that's not true! You were ALL a big help to me!

Japanese: （いなかったくせに、 ムチャ言うなよ‥‥）
English: (She shouldn't take it so personally. It's not like she was exactly "free" to help...)

Japanese: 半年ぶりにお会いすると、 やはり見ちがえますね。
English: You've certainly changed since I last saw you 7 months ago.

Japanese: そ、そうですか？
English: R-Really?

Japanese: たくましくなったような 気がしますよ。
English: Yes, you look like you've bulked up a little.

Japanese: （そう言われてみると‥‥）
English: (Well, now that she mentions it...)

Japanese: （急にたくましく なったような気がしてきた）
English: (I guess I have gotten a little stronger...)

Japanese: ‥‥タンジュンだなあ、 なるほどくんは。
English: You're such a dork, Nick.

Japanese: すみません‥‥ムカシほど カオが広くないものですから。
English: I'm sorry, I don't know as many people as I used to.

Japanese: 特に参考になりそうなコトは お話しできませんわ。
English: So there really isn't anything I can tell you about that person.

Japanese: あら‥‥。もう８時すぎ。 みなさん、お食事はまだですよね？
English: Oh, look! It's already 8:00! Have you all eaten yet?

Japanese: ええ。まあ‥‥。
English: Um, well actually...

Japanese: １２階のレストランに、 予約を入れていますの。
English: I've already made a reservation at the restaurant on the 12th floor.

Japanese: ごいっしょにディナーを、 と思いまして。
English: Why don't we all dine together?

Japanese: わお。 いいですねー、でぃなー。
English: Ooh! I like the sound of that! "Dine"... It sounds so fancy!

Japanese: わたくし、 お子さまランチがいいです！
English: I'll have the "Kids Lunch"!

Japanese: ふっふっふっ。はみちゃん。 ランチはおヒルに食べるものだよ。
English: Ha ha ha. Pearly, it's dinner, not lunch.

Japanese: 今夜はグッとオトナっぽく、 お子さまディナーで行こうよ！
English: Why don't you go for it and really eat your fill with a "Kid's Dinner"?

Japanese: はいっ！　じゃあ、 お子さまディナーで！
English: OK! In that case, one "Kid's Dinner" for me!

Japanese: ふふ。じゃあ、行きましょうか。
English: Ha ha. Shall we head upstairs, then?

Japanese: ‥‥というわけで、 あとは公開日を待つばかり。
English: After that pleasant evening, all that remained was to wait for the exhibition's opening.

Japanese: 霧緒さんの元気なカオも見られて、 それは楽しい晩だった。
English: It was wonderful seeing Ms. Andrews looking so happy.

Japanese: いったい‥‥ だれが想像できただろう。
English: None of us could have imagined...

Japanese: ‥‥その翌日、 あんな事件が起こるなんて‥‥
English: That the very next day, something terrible would happen...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 高菱屋　地下倉庫
English: October 12 Lordly Tailor Basement Warehouse

Japanese: 何度来ても、ブキミなところだね。 ‥‥ちょっと、サムケがするよ。
English: No matter how many times we come here, this place always gives me the chills.

Japanese: クーラーが入ってるからな。 美術品を管理するためだろうね。
English: That's probably because it's air-conditioned... To protect the art pieces, you know.

Japanese: ‥‥やあれ！ 我が混沌の宴へようこそ‥‥
English: ...Welcome! To my private little "Banquet of Chaos".

Japanese: （なな、なんだなんだ。 “やあれ！”って‥‥）
English: (Huh? What the heck does THAT mean...?)

Japanese: 大したもてなしもできませぬが‥‥ どうぞ、ごゆるりと。
English: I'm afraid I have very little to offer, but please... make yourself at home.

Japanese: あ‥‥あの。 あなたさまは‥‥？
English: Err, umm... Who are you anyway...?

Japanese: ‥‥シッ！　ダマって！
English: SHHH! SILENCE!

Japanese: クックックックックッ‥‥ どうやら、見えたようですな。
English: Hee hee hee hee hee hee... Now I see! It's all becoming clear!

Japanese: なな、なんですか？
English: Wh-What do you see?

Japanese: ‥‥ズヴァリ！ 美しきスイリが我にささやく真実。
English: ...Zvarri! The truth has once again been elegantly revealed to me!

Japanese: 貴方がたは、弁護士と‥‥おそらく 霊媒師。‥‥それも、半人前の！
English: A lawyer, and... a spirit medium. Neither very advanced at their trade!

Japanese: ‥‥ちがいますかな？
English: ...Am I wrong?

Japanese: きゃわあっ！ どど、どうしてわかったんですか！
English: Yikes! Th-That's scary! H-How did you know that!?

Japanese: “どうして”‥‥？　そんな問いに なんのイミがありましょうや。
English: "How" you say? The universal skeptic would say that how is nothing but a question of why.

Japanese: 我らは、あえかなる時の流れを 旅する、うたかたのユメのごとし。
English: I am a traveler of both time and space, and a swimmer of dreams.

Japanese: ‥‥ナニ言ってるかゼンゼン わかんないよ、なるほどくん‥‥
English: What is he talking about? I can't make heads or tails of it...

Japanese: さあれ、好敵手に名乗りを上げるは 紳士の第一条件。
English: Without further ado, let me fulfill what should be the first duty of any gentleman...

Japanese: ‥‥申しおくれました。我が名は 星威岳　哀牙（ほしいだけあいが）
English: ...and introduce myself. The name is Atmey... Luke Atmey!

Japanese: 名探偵‥‥です。
English: ...Ace Detective.

Japanese: は、はあ‥‥
English: Oh... Umm... Hi.

Japanese: （ダメだよ、なるほどくん！ ここは、カマしておかないと！）
English: Don't let him intimidate you, Nick! Stand up and show him what you're made of!

Japanese: え、ええと‥‥成歩堂　龍一です。 ‥‥め、名弁護士です。
English: Err, umm... The name's Wright. Phoenix Wright... ...Ace Attorney.

Japanese: あたし、綾里　真宵です！ 名霊媒師でおなじみの。
English: I'm Maya Fey! Ace Spirit Medium!

Japanese: わたくしは‥‥ 綾里　春美と申します。
English: And I'm, ummm... Pearl Fey.

Japanese: 真宵さまの名イトコで、 霊媒師の‥‥名タマゴ、です。
English: I'm Mystic Maya's cousin and umm... spirit medium Ace Apprentice.

Japanese: よろしい！
English: Excellent!

Japanese: さぁ、これよりゲエムを スタアトしようではありませぬか！
English: Well then, shall we begin our little game?

Japanese: （“ゲエム”と来たか‥‥）
English: ("Game"? Why does the phrase "one short of a baker's dozen" come to mind with this guy?)

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、弁護士殿。
English: ...Well, Sir Lawyer.

Japanese: 貴方がたは、シンケンに 怪人を追っているとお見受けした。
English: I've been told that you too are pursuing the mysterious thief.

Japanese: ‥‥いやあ。 ベツに、それほどでも
English: ...Well, I'm not sure would say that exac--

Japanese: そうなんです！
English: That's right!

Japanese: あたしたち、なんとしてもツボを 見つけだすつもりですから！
English: We're going to find the Sacred Urn no matter what we have to do!

Japanese: ‥‥よろしい。
English: ...Excellent.

Japanese: 貴方がたを信用して、我は 他の場所を調査します。
English: I will trust you. I permit you to take a look around while I investigate elsewhere.

Japanese: 何か見つけたら、 我が事務所へ参るがよいでしょう！
English: If, perchance, you should discover something of value, return then to my office...

Japanese: ほまれ高き地上の楽園、 《哀牙探偵事務所》へ！
English: To my office of Earthly Delights, the "Atmey Detective Agency"!

Japanese: ち、地上の楽園‥‥
English: Of-Office of Earthly D-Delights...?

Japanese: それでは。あとは たのみましたぞ、弁護士殿！
English: Well then Sir Lawyer, if you'll excuse me, I have much to do!

Japanese: いや、ちょっと‥‥！
English: W-Wait a minute...!

Japanese: 行ってしまわれましたね。
English: He's gone.

Japanese: コマるな、あとをたのまれても。
English: I don't like the idea of doing this guy's work for him.

Japanese: ま。ま。いいじゃない、 なるほどくん。
English: Come on, Nick. Don't worry about it. It's no big deal.

Japanese: 探偵さんの気が変わらないウチに、 調べまくっちゃおうよ！
English: Let's hurry up and investigate before he changes his mind!

Japanese: あとで、たんていさんの事務所にも 連れていってくださいね！
English: I wanna go to his office, too! Take me with you, OK?

Japanese: おお、弁護士殿！　いささか、 出すぎた行為ではないかな？
English: Tsk! Sir Lawyer! Please refrain from such crude behavior.

Japanese: え‥‥な、なんですか？
English: Huh? Wh-What do you mean?

Japanese: ‥‥犯罪者こそは、芸術家！ その犯行現場は、彼らの芸術品！
English: This criminal is an artist! The crime scene is the canvas upon which he paints!

Japanese: 名探偵以外、そのカンヴァスの美を 読みとることは不可能！
English: Only I, Ace Detective Atmey, have an eye which is educated enough to read the signs!

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどくん。 通訳をおねがい。
English: ...Could you translate that for me, Nick?

Japanese: “勝手にさわるな、シロートめ！” ってトコかな。
English: I think he pretty much said, "Don't touch anything amateurs!"

Japanese: この場を調べたいのであれば！ 哀牙に示すべきなのですよ！
English: If there's something you wish to know about, you have but to ask Atmey!

Japanese: ‥‥我がライヴァル・仮面マスク について、どれだけ知っているか！
English: ...I know everything there is to know about my rival, Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: （どうすりゃいいんだよ‥‥）
English: (What do I do now...?)

Japanese: ゴクヒ情報とか、ないの？ マニアしか知らないような‥‥
English: Does he have any top-secret information? Stuff that only a real crime buff would know?

Japanese: （‥‥名探偵の話を聞きながら 考えてみるか‥‥）
English: (I'll think about what to ask while I listen to what Mr. Ace Detective has to say...)

Japanese: カッコイイなー、供子さま。
English: This statue of Mystic Ami is sooo cool.

Japanese: ウチの事務所にもほしいよねー、 あたしの黄金像。
English: I want a gold statue of myself! We could put it in the office!

Japanese: 《成歩堂法律事務所》だぞ。 置くなら、ぼくの黄金像だろ？
English: Hey! It's WRIGHT & Co.! How about a gold statue of ME?

Japanese: ‥‥あの、なるほどくん。 それより、気になるのですけど‥‥
English: Um, Mr. Nick? I'm sorry, but I noticed something strange...

Japanese: ん？　どうしたの、春美ちゃん。
English: Huh? What is it, Pearls?

Japanese: 供子さまの像‥‥ゆうべも、 あんなところにありましたか？
English: The statue of Mystic Ami... Was this where it was last night?
Ah...

Japanese: たしかゆうべは‥‥もっと、扉に 近い場所に立っていたような‥‥。
English: I'm pretty sure it was closer to the door...

Japanese: そ、そういえば‥‥
English: Y-Yeah, I think you're right.

Japanese: やるね、はみちゃん！ さっすが、あたしの名イトコ！
English: Great job, Pearly! I wouldn't expect anything less from my Ace Cousin!

Japanese: （事件があった夜、動かされた 黄金像、か‥‥）
English: (So the statue was moved on the night of the crime...)

Japanese: 《綾里　供子の黄金像》の データを書きなおした。
English: Ami Fey's Golden Statue updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: これ、スゴいよねー。 “しちしとう”とか言ったっけ。
English: Wow, this is awesome. What did Ms. Andrews say it was called again?

Japanese: 七支刀。‥‥７つに 枝わかれしたカタナだからね。
English: "Shichishito". It's literally "7 Branch Sword" in Japanese. Or so says Ms. Andrews.

Japanese: お。‥‥けっこう重いぞ。
English: Whoa... It's pretty heavy.

Japanese: 『ひと突きで７回イタい』‥‥か。 大ヒット商品になるかも！
English: "One hit, seven times the fun!" Pretty catchy, huh? I bet it'd sell like hotcakes!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。わたくし、 それより、気になるのですけど‥‥
English: Um, there's something about it that bothers me...

Japanese: ほほお！　今日はサエてるねー、 はみちゃん。
English: Wow! You're really firing on all cylinders today, Pearly!

Japanese: このカタナ‥‥ゆうべは 供子さまが持ってましたよね。
English: This sword... Wasn't Mystic Ami holding it last night?
Ah...

Japanese: それに‥‥ゆうべは、カタナは 曲がっていませんでした。
English: And another thing... The sword wasn't bent last night like it is now.

Japanese: た、たしかに‥‥
English: Y-You're right...

Japanese: やるね、はみちゃん！ さっすが、霊媒師の名タマゴ！
English: Awesome, Pearly! I wouldn't expect any less from my little Ace Apprentice!

Japanese: （このカタナが曲がった、 ということは‥‥）
English: (The sword is bent, so that must mean...)

Japanese: （ゆうべコイツは“使われた”と いうことになる‥‥）
English: (Last night, someone "used it" somehow...)

Japanese: あ。もしかしたら アレじゃないかな！
English: Aha. I just thought of something!

Japanese: 哀牙探偵が、仮面マスクを 思いっきり殴った、とか。
English: Maybe Detective Atmey smashed Mask☆DeMasque with it.

Japanese: こんな感じで！
English: Like this!

Japanese: いてええええぇぇェェッ！ ななな、何するんだよ！
English: YEOOOOOUCCCH!! WHAT THE HECK!

Japanese: どう？　はみちゃん。
English: What do you think, Pearly?

Japanese: 先ほどより、ちょっと 曲がったような気がします。
English: I think it's even more bent than it was before.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥ アタマがクラクラする‥‥）
English: (Oh man... My head... My HAIR!)

Japanese: 証拠品《七支刀》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Shichishito added to the Court Record.

Japanese: ダメだよなるほどくん。 ペンキなんかこぼしちゃ。
English: Look what you did, Nick! You spilled paint over here!

Japanese: ぼくじゃないよ！
English: I didn't do that!

Japanese: だって、ゆうべはなかったもん。 こんなハデなシミ。
English: Well it wasn't here last night. I would have noticed a color this outrageous.

Japanese: でも‥‥このペンキ、 もうすっかり乾いていますよ。
English: But this paint has already dried up.

Japanese: ダメだよなるほどくん。 乾く前にふかなくちゃ。
English: Well then, you should have cleaned it up before it dried, Nick!

Japanese: ペンキがそんなにすぐ 乾くかよ！
English: Paint doesn't dry that fast! Besides, I told you it wasn't me!

Japanese: （こぼしてから、数日は たっているみたいだな‥‥）
English: (It looks like it's been several days since it was spilt.)

Japanese: ‥‥それに、ここ‥‥。
English: ...Hey, look at this!

Japanese: なんですか？
English: What is it?

Japanese: シミの左下が‥‥ 少し、欠けているぞ。
English: There's some kind of shape left in the bottom left side of this stain.

Japanese: あれ。‥‥ホントだ。 なんでだろ‥‥。
English: Huh? ...You're right. I wonder what it could be...?

Japanese: 知ってる？　はみちゃん。 パソコンだよ、これ。
English: Hey, Pearly, do you know what this is? It's a computer!

Japanese: はあ、ぱそこん‥‥。 とても、かわいらしい名前ですね。
English: A "kom-pu-ter"...? Oh! I've heard of those! It has such a cute name!

Japanese: どうやら、あの防犯カメラの データを管理しているみたいだ。
English: It looks like this computer is for storing the data from the security camera.

Japanese: だれかが倉庫に出入りすると、 あのカメラが写真を撮るんだ。
English: When someone comes in or out of the warehouse, the camera takes a photo of them.

Japanese: たんていさんがしかけた 《わな》かもしれませんね。
English: It's probably the "trap" that the detective set up.

Japanese: ちょっと、ゆうべの データを調べてみようよ。
English: Let's see if we can find last night's data on this thing.

Japanese: 仮面マスクが 写ってるかもしれないじゃない！
English: We might even find a photo of Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ‥‥そ、そうだね。 （ニガテなんだよな、キカイ‥‥）
English: Hmm, let see here... (Man, I really stink at working these things...)

Japanese: どうですか？　なるほどくん。 アセびっしょりですけど‥‥
English: What's wrong, Mr. Nick? You're sweating an awful lot.

Japanese: ‥‥写真を見る方法が わからない‥‥
English: ...I don't know how to pull up the photos...

Japanese: 弱いなー、なるほどくん。 “はいてく”に。
English: I had no idea you were so pathetic with high-tech stuff, Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥でも、カメラのデータは プリントアウトできたよ。
English: ...Yeah, well I managed to print out the camera data anyway.

Japanese: お！　ころんでもタダじゃ 起きないね、なるほどくん！
English: I guess you have to learn to crawl before you can walk, right, Nick?

Japanese: これによると、ゆうべカメラは １回、作動しているみたいだ。
English: Yeah, sure... Anyway, it looks like the camera only went off once last night.

Japanese: 証拠品《カメラのデータ》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Camera Data added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 防犯カメラだ。 あの大きな扉をとらえている。
English: It's a security camera. It's trained on the big door to the storeroom.

Japanese: 倉庫に出入りする人間を チェックしてるのだろう。
English: It's set up to take a photo of anyone that goes in or out.

Japanese: “ぼうはんカメラ”‥‥ですか。
English: A "se-kure-ity camera"...?

Japanese: ほらほら。あの扉の前に立てば、 はみちゃんも写るよ、カメラ。
English: Yeah, go stand in front of that door and it'll snap your photo too, Pearly.

Japanese: あ、わたくし。やはり、ニッコリ したほうがよろしいのでしょうか。
English: Umm... Mystic Maya... Do you think I should smile?

Japanese: 犯人を写すカメラだからね。 キョーアクなカオがいいと思うよ。
English: Nah. It's for criminals, so I think you should make a really scary face!

Japanese: こ、こうでしょうか‥‥
English: L-Like this...?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 あの大きな扉はなんですか？
English: Mr. Nick, what's that big door over there?

Japanese: 扉の向こうが倉庫に なっているんだ。
English: It looks like it's the door to the actual storeroom.

Japanese: いろんな大きさのハコが こっちから見えるよ。
English: I can see lots of big boxes and stuff back there.

Japanese: 倉院のツボ、あそこに しまってあったんだよね‥‥。
English: I guess that's where they were keeping the Sacred Urn.

Japanese: 入っちゃダメだぞ。
English: Remember, Maya. Ms. Andrews said we're not allowed in there.

Japanese: あそこに捨てられてる 粗末な木バコは、もしかして‥‥？
English: That pathetic-looking wooden box...

Japanese: あっ！　倉院のツボを しまっておいたハコだ！
English: Ahh!! That's the box that had the Sacred Urn in it!

Japanese: さわっちゃダメだよ。犯人の 手がかりが残ってるかもしれない。
English: Don't touch it! There may be clues to the thief's identity on there!

Japanese: ううう‥‥あたしが一生懸命 作ったのに‥‥
English: Grrr... I worked so hard to make that box!

Japanese: え！　真宵ちゃんが作ったの？ あの粗末な木バコ‥‥
English: Huh!? You MADE that poor excuse for a box, Maya?

Japanese: ソマツって言うな！
English: What's that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: ツボはいつも、お屋敷の ろうかにかざってありましたから。
English: That urn has always been on its stand in the Winding Way of Fey Manor.

Japanese: ちょうどいいハコが なかったのです。
English: So there was no box suitable for it, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: 自信作だったのになあ‥‥。
English: And I-I was so proud of it... *sniffle sniffle*

Japanese: （仮面マスク‥‥いっしょに 盗んであげればよかったのに）
English: (Poor Maya. Maybe it'd have been better if Mask☆DeMasque had taken the box, too...)

Japanese: あの‥‥そんなに 名探偵なんですか？
English: Umm... So are you really as famous as you say?

Japanese: これはしたり、弁護士殿！ ユウモアのセンスがおありだ。
English: Of course! For a lawyer, you have a rather shocking lack of knowledge about the world.

Japanese: 名探偵・哀牙の名を知らぬ者が まだ、この街に残されていたとは！
English: I had no idea anyone was left in this city that had not heard of me! Look at me!

Japanese: 名探偵・星威岳　哀牙！ そして、怪人☆仮面マスク！
English: I am Mask☆DeMasque's arch-enemy, the Ace Detective Luke Atmey!

Japanese: 街は今、この２大巨人の一騎撃ちに そう！　クギづけなのだッ！
English: At this moment, there are two mighty figures which loom large over this grand city!

Japanese: 魑魅魍魎の跳梁跋扈する頻闇の帷が 帝都を包む宵、決闘の緞帳が開く！
English: One embodies the nefarious forces of darkness. The other the angelic cherubs of light!

Japanese: ‥‥読めないし、イミもゼンゼン わかんないよ、なるほどくん‥‥
English: ...Boy, now I'm really lost. What is he saying, Nick?

Japanese: 我は、仮面マスクの２回目の犯行 から、独自の調査を開始しました。
English: I have been personally on the heels of this villain since his first crime.

Japanese: 彼の手口をスイリして‥‥ 前回の犯行で、栄光を我が手に！
English: After learning this criminal's patterns, I thwarted his very latest loathsome larceny!

Japanese: ヤツのエモノ《メジーナ婦人像》を 犯行現場にて、取り戻したのです！
English: I even returned the target of his tyranny, the "Portrait of Mejeena" to the crime scene!

Japanese: たったヒトリで！ あの仮面マスクから！
English: And I did it all by myself! I took it back from Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: この肉体派の脳ミソと、 頭脳派の腕っぷしで！
English: With my brawny mind and my brilliant muscles!

Japanese: ニュースでもやってたよ。 “街のスゴいヤツ”のコーナーで。
English: Yeah! They mentioned it on the news, in the "Great People of the Town" segment.

Japanese: 美術館のヒトから、スゴい 宝石をもらったんだって！
English: They said that the museum gave you an incredible jewel as a reward!

Japanese: ‥‥哀牙の取り戻したエモノに くらべれば、安いものですよ‥‥
English: ...Well, compared to the treasure that I retrieved for them, it's not much.

Japanese: （ジマンするだけのコトは ある、ってわけか‥‥）
English: (So... in the end, the only thing that makes you great is your own self-praise...)

Japanese: 怪人のこと、聞かせてください！ ‥‥あの、手みじかに。
English: Oh! Tell us what you know about Mask☆DeMasque! ...And keep it simple, please!

Japanese: 我が生涯の好敵手にしてライヴァル ‥‥そういったところですかな。
English: Like about how I have devoted my life to thwarting his dastardly deeds?

Japanese: ゆうべも、このデパアトにて 決闘の火花を散らしたワケです。
English: Last night, here in the bowels of this store, we were locked in a most glorious battle.

Japanese: なるほど‥‥って、 ちょっと待ってください！
English: I see... ...Huh!? Hang on a second!

Japanese: あなた、ここにいたんですか？ ゆうべ、この高菱屋に！
English: You were here last night? Here? In Lordly Tailor!?

Japanese: 当然！　仮面マスクの行くところ、 哀牙のワナが華麗にキバをむく！
English: Naturally! Wherever DeMasque goes, you will find Atmey laying his elegant traps.

Japanese: （‥‥そして、華麗に 取り逃がしたワケか‥‥）
English: (Elegant traps? More like DeMasque out-eleganced you and made a beautiful getaway.)

Japanese: 今回はこの哀牙、敵にハナを 持たせてやったワケですな。
English: But this time, I allowed him to escape with the illusion of victory.

Japanese: （ゆうべのコト、詳しく 聞いたほうがいいかな‥‥）
English: (I wonder if I should ask him about what happened last night in more detail...)

Japanese: あの‥‥ゆうべのこと、 聞かせてもらえますか？
English: Umm... Can you tell us exactly what happened here last night?

Japanese: よいでしょう。“名物”どうし、 かくしゴトはよくないですからな。
English: But of course. After all, I always say there should be no secrets between "aces".

Japanese: （『名探偵』の“名”って、 “名物”のコトだったのか‥‥）
English: (Flattery will get you nowhere, Pinocchio...)

Japanese: 我が名探偵が、警備の依頼を 受けたのは、今から２０日前。
English: I first received the request for my services 20 days ago.

Japanese: 怪人の予告状が届く、 １０日ほど前のこと‥‥。
English: And it was 10 days ago that we received Mask☆DeMasque's calling card...

Japanese: え！　予告状が来る前から 警備をたのまれたんですか！
English: Huh? You were hired BEFORE the calling card even arrived?

Japanese: 我が依頼人は、用心深く リコウなおじょうさんでしたな。
English: Yes. You see, the person who hired me is quite rigorous and thorough.

Japanese: （‥‥霧緒さんか‥‥）
English: (That's Adrian for you...)

Japanese: 警備を始めてから１０日後！ 彼女の悪い予感は当たった！
English: A mere 10 days after hiring me, her worst premonitions were realized!

Japanese: 予告状が届いたんですね！
English: A calling card arrived, right?

Japanese: 我が名探偵は、 この倉庫にワナを張った！
English: Yes! So, Ace Detective that I am, I set a trap for the thief right in the warehouse.

Japanese: そして‥‥ただひとり、 怪人を待ったのです！
English: And waited for him... alone.

Japanese: “ひとり”‥‥って、どうして 警察に知らせなかったんですか？
English: Alone? ...But why? Why didn't you go to the police for help?

Japanese: ナンセンスですな、霊媒師殿。 無能のヤカラに、何ができます？
English: Ridiculous! Why would I seek help from such a singularly useless group of nincompoops?

Japanese: （自分だって、ケッキョク 逃げられたクセに‥‥）
English: (You've got a lot of nerve. After all, it was you that let DeMasque get away.)

Japanese: 我はつねに、人前に姿は 見せず、かくれているのです。
English: I always operate alone and concealed. Out of the sight of others.

Japanese: かくれている‥‥？
English: "Corn Sealed"...?

Japanese: さよう。張りこんでいる刑事も、 この名探偵の存在は知らぬハズ。
English: Precisely! Even the guards on duty here were unaware of my presence.

Japanese: 《敵をあざむくには、味方から》 これは、哀牙が考えたコトバです。
English: "To fool your enemies, first you must fool your friends." It's my own original proverb!

Japanese: それで、ゆうべのコトですけど‥‥
English: Anyway, back to what happened last night...

Japanese: この哀牙は、この地下倉庫に 身をひそめて、ヤツを待っていた。
English: As I was saying, I hid here in the warehouse and waited for the thief to arrive.

Japanese: ゆうべ‥‥あの大きな扉を 通った者はいなかった！
English: But I swear to you... Not a solitary soul came through that entrance!

Japanese: それなのに‥‥ ツボは消えてしまったのです。
English: Nevertheless... The Sacred Urn disappeared!

Japanese: そ、そんなバカな！ いったい、どうやって‥‥？
English: B-But that's impossible! How could it just disappear!?

Japanese: “どうやって”‥‥？ ナンセンスですな、弁護士殿。
English: "How?" you ask? Don't be ridiculous, Sir Lawyer.

Japanese: それがわかっていれば、 こんなところにいるイミはなし！
English: If I knew the answer to that question, I wouldn't be here!

Japanese: （ムネを張って言うコトかよ‥‥）
English: (For someone who seriously messed up his own stakeout, he's rather full of himself.)

Japanese: つまり‥‥ゆうべ、あの扉は だれも通っていないのに‥‥、
English: So you're saying that even though on one came through those doors last night...

Japanese: 倉庫の中から、ツボは 消え失せた、ということですね。
English: ...our precious urn still somehow vanished from under your nose?

Japanese: （そ、そんなバカな‥‥）
English: (Th-That's just not possible!)

Japanese: このコンピュータのデータに よれば‥‥
English: According to the computer data...

Japanese: ゆうべ、たしかに扉を通った 人物がいる、ってことだよな。
English: ...someone did in fact go through those big doors last night, right?

Japanese: うん。シャッターが１回、 作動しているからね。
English: Yeah, I guess so. The shutter did go off once, you know.

Japanese: このカメラ、あの探偵さんが 持ってきたのかな。
English: I wonder if that detective brought this camera in with him?

Japanese: そうなんじゃない。 ちょっと、見てみようか。
English: Good question! Let's take a look at it.

Japanese: ‥‥アレ。カメラに 《高菱屋・備品》って書いてある。
English: ...Huh? It says "Property of Lordly Tailor" on it.

Japanese: なんだよ、借り物か。
English: So it's borrowed...?

Japanese: ホント、ナニかとウサンくさい 探偵さんだねえ‥‥。
English: Kind of strange for an "Ace Detective", don't you think?

Japanese: ゆうべ、霧緒さんと見たときは、 こんなトコロにはなかったね。
English: When we were here with Ms. Andrews last night, the statue wasn't where it is now.

Japanese: うん。たしかに、扉の すぐ横に立っていたよ。
English: Yeah, it was right next to the door, wasn't it?

Japanese: 動かしたとしたら‥‥ 犯人だよな、たぶん。
English: Well, if someone moved it, it was probably the criminal.

Japanese: 倉庫に入るとき、視線を感じた のかな。供子さまの。
English: Maybe he didn't like being watched by Mystic Ami while he stole the urn.

Japanese: ‥‥コワいこと、言うなよ。
English: ...Hey! Cut it out! You're giving me the creeps with that kind of talk!

Japanese: あたしたちのツボ、 この扉の向こうにあったんだよね。
English: Our precious urn was right there on the other side of these doors.

Japanese: うん。‥‥そして、防犯カメラの データによれば‥‥
English: Yeah, and according to the security camera data...

Japanese: ゆうべ、ここに入ったヤツが いるんだよね。
English: Someone was in there last night.

Japanese: ううう‥‥見つけたら、 どうしてくれよう‥‥！
English: Grrr... What I'd like to do to him...!

Japanese: なんかあたし、 気に入っちゃったよ、これ。
English: I'm really starting to like this thing. It's so cool-looking.

Japanese: コシにさして 街をカッポしてみたいねえ。
English: I'd like to strap it to my waist and walk around town like a samurai.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事に タイホされるぞ。
English: Careful. Gumshoe will lock you up and throw away the key if you're caught.

Japanese: 見た感じ、乾いてから 数日はたっているみたいだ。
English: It looks to me like it's been dry for several days.

Japanese: このペンキの跡も、 何かとナゾが多いよねー。
English: There's something suspicious about this paint mark.

Japanese: シミの左下が欠けてるし、 なぜかピンクだし。
English: The bottom left part of it is shaped oddly and it's shocking pink.

Japanese: ‥‥ペンキの色はこの際、 どうでもいいと思うけど。
English: ...I don't see how the color of the paint is in any way important.

Japanese: あそこに捨てられてる 粗末な木バコは、もしかして‥‥？
English: That pathetic-looking wooden box...

Japanese: あっ！　倉院のツボを しまっておいたハコだ！
English: Ahh!! That's the box that had the Sacred Urn in it!

Japanese: さわっちゃダメだよ。犯人の 手がかりが残ってるかもしれない。
English: Don't touch it! There may be clues to the thief's identity on it!

Japanese: ううう‥‥あたしが一生懸命 作ったのに‥‥
English: Grrr... I worked so hard to make that box!

Japanese: え！　真宵ちゃんが作ったの？ あの粗末な木バコ‥‥
English: Huh!? You MADE that poor excuse for a box, Maya?

Japanese: ソマツって言うな！
English: What's that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: なんで捨てちゃったのかな、犯人。 自信作だったのに‥‥。
English: And I-I was so proud of it... *sniffle sniffle*

Japanese: （仮面マスク‥‥いっしょに 盗んであげればよかったのに）
English: (Poor Maya. Maybe it'd have been better if Mask☆DeMasque had taken the box, too...)

Japanese: このパソコン‥‥上にある カメラの管理をしているみたいだ。
English: This computer... It looks like it's hooked up to the security camera above.

Japanese: え。どういうこと？
English: And that means what?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ だれかが倉庫に出入りすると、
English: The camera is set up to take a photo of anyone that goes in or out of the storeroom...

Japanese: あのカメラが写真を撮るんだ。 そのデータを管理しているわけ。
English: Then the data from the camera gets uploaded to this computer here. That's what.

Japanese: はああ。 “はいてく”だねえ‥‥。
English: I see... That's some pretty high-tech stuff.

Japanese: 大きな看板の一部みたいだ。 《バー》って書いてある。
English: It looks like a part of a big signboard. All I can read on it is the "bur".

Japanese: あ、わかった！ “ハンバーグ”じゃないかな。
English: Aha! I've got it! Maybe it's supposed to say, "Hamburger"!

Japanese: こんなにデカく“ハンバーグ”って 書いて、どうするんだよ。
English: Why would anyone write "Hamburger" that big?

Japanese: “世界ハンバーグ祭り”とか。
English: Like, maybe for the "World Hamburger Festival" or something?

Japanese: ちがうと思うけどなあ。
English: I kinda doubt it.

Japanese: あ、わかった！ “ラーメン”じゃないかな！
English: Aha! I've got it! Maybe it said, "Spaghetti"!

Japanese: ‥‥さっきから 食べものばっかりだな。
English: ...Why are you talking about food? Oh, wait. That's normal for you.

Japanese: そもそも“ラー”じゃなくて “バー”だぞ！
English: Anyway, it says, "bur". There's no way it could be "Spaghetti".

Japanese: 書きまちがえた、とか。 “みそラーメン祭り”。‥‥どう？
English: Well, maybe it was a typo! It might have said, "Spaghetti Festival". What do you think?

Japanese: ‥‥楽しそうだとは思うよ。
English: OK to me... Besides, Spaghetti is the only thing more tangled than your reasoning.

Japanese: あ、ハシゴだ。
English: Look, a ladder!

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: That's a "step"-ladder.

Japanese: どうちがうの？ 同じようなものじゃない。
English: So? What's the difference?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ようよ、 なるほどくん。
English: You need to stop judging things based on narrow-minded cultural assumptions, Nick!

Japanese: ははは。
English: R-Right... sorry.

Japanese: （‥‥どこかで、同じような 会話をかわしたような‥‥）
English: (Why do I feel like we've had this exact same conversation before somewhere...?)

Japanese: 《整理整とん》って書いてある。
English: It says "ORGANI".

Japanese: ひらがなだから、 なるほどくんでも安心だね。
English: No it doesn't, Nick! It says, "ORGANIZE"! You just can't see the last two letters.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、そのワリに 散らかってるな。
English: Yeah, well I guess no one else could read it either, because this place is a mess.

Japanese: 《せいりせいとん》って 書くべきだったね。
English: Yeah. You must feel right at home, huh?

Japanese: そういえばさっき、ぼくの 職業をピタリと当てましたよね。
English: That's pretty amazing the way you were able to tell what I do for a living.

Japanese: あいや、弁護士殿。 ほんの初歩ですよ、初歩。
English: Oh, it's elementary, my dear Wright, elementary.

Japanese: よろしいか。まずは貴方の髪型。 ‥‥後ろ向きにトガっている。
English: If you're really interested, the first clue was the hair. ...It points backwards.

Japanese: アキラカに闇討ちに対する用心だ。 闇討ちといえば犯罪人。
English: It shows you're prepared to take on an attack. Such as from a criminal, for example.

Japanese: 服装から刑事ではないことが わかるから、残るは検事か弁護士。
English: From your clothes, I knew you weren't a policeman, so I deduced you were a lawyer.

Japanese: あとはヒラメキとカンでズヴァリ！ ‥‥真実がこの手に、１つ。
English: After a combination of cold logic and intuition... Zvarri! Only the truth remains.

Japanese: （最後はカンかよ‥‥）
English: (Wow, impressive...)

Japanese: エリにつけてる弁護士バッジを 見れば、イッパツなのに‥‥
English: Or maybe he just took a look at the attorney's badge on your lapel, genius.

Japanese: あの。あの。‥‥なんであたしが 霊媒師だってわかったんですか！
English: Umm... So how did you know I was a spirit medium?

Japanese: ‥‥たやすいことですよ。
English: Oh, that was child's play.

Japanese: まずは貴方の髪型。 これまたフツーではない。
English: First, your hair. It's not of the type one sees every day.

Japanese: そして、珍にして妙なる、 そのヘンなカッコウ。
English: Both rare and enchanting... Almost... otherworldly.

Japanese: 占い師、もしくは霊媒師である 可能性がきわめて高い。
English: I thought it likely that you were either a fortune teller or a spirit medium.

Japanese: さらに、占い師としての知性が 感じられないとなれば‥‥
English: But I didn't quite detect the wisdom one usually sees in a fortune teller...

Japanese: あとは、ズヴァリ！ ‥‥真実がこの手に、もう１つ。
English: And so... Zvarri! That left only one possibility.

Japanese: 秘宝展のポスターに、写真入りで 紹介されているのに‥‥。
English: Well, they also have a bio about me on the Treasure Exhibit poster...

Japanese: この人物は‥‥ズヴァリ！ 小学生ですな。
English: As for this child... Zvarri! Elementary school student.

Japanese: まあ！　当たりですっ！
English: Wow! That's right!

Japanese: どうやら、ここ数年以内に、 中学生になろうとしている‥‥
English: I can also see that you will be a middle-school student within the next few years!

Japanese: そ、そんなコトまで！
English: Th-That's incredible!

Japanese: （聞いてるこっちが 恥ずかしくなるな‥‥）
English: (No doubt about it. This guy is good!)

Japanese: ‥‥それを涼しいカオで言って のけるのが、名探偵なのですよ。
English: Now you can see how I have earned my reputation as an Ace Detective.

Japanese: この人物は‥‥かなり、 ビジンですな。
English: This woman... She is a true beauty.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: ...You don't say.

Japanese: 今度、紹介していただきたい。
English: I would be honored if you would introduce her to me.

Japanese: いや、弁護士殿。これはあくまで、 職業的キョーミですぞ！
English: I assure you, Sir Lawyer, that my interest is strictly professional!

Japanese: なんか、ちょっと いかがわしいね、なるほどくん。
English: Eww, this guy's even creepier than I thought.

Japanese: そのほのかなカオリこそが、 名探偵の第一条件なのですよ！
English: I assure you, my dear, all Ace Detectives are perfect gentlemen!

Japanese: ‥‥ああ、さっき 会いましたよ、この刑事なら。
English: ...Oh, him. Yes, well I saw him earlier.

Japanese: 怪人の犯行に毎回カオを出し、 毎回、取り逃がすズッコケ刑事。
English: That wretched detective has let the thief slip through his fingers every single time.

Japanese: ずっこけ‥‥
English: Wretched, huh...

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えると、この人物も 怪人の一味と考えてもよい。
English: ...Now that I think about it, I wonder if they aren't working together.
Huh...?

Japanese: “善意の共犯者”ですな。 我に言わせれば。
English: If you ask me, Luke Atmey, I believe the two of them are "co-conspirators".

Japanese: イトノコさんが聞いたら 泣いちゃうね、きっと。
English: If Detective Gumshoe heard that, he'd probably cry.

Japanese: ナイーブなオトコだからなあ。
English: He is a surprisingly sensitive guy...

Japanese: この人物は‥‥ズヴァリ！ 名探偵、ではないですかな？
English: This person is... Zvarri! A great Ace Detective! Am I correct?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくに問いかけられても‥‥
English: Um, but I was the one asking you...

Japanese: この写真だけでは、データ不足で 断言はできませんが‥‥
English: This photo simply isn't enough information. I'm afraid I can't be absolutely certain...

Japanese: このリコウそうな目、 そうとうなヤリ手と見ましたぞ！
English: But the intelligence in those eyes indicates a man of deep wisdom and insight.

Japanese: ‥‥ホンキで言ってるよ、 なるほどくん。
English: Stop messing around, Nick, and just ask him already!

Japanese: ちょっとコワくなってきた。
English: B-But this guy is scary.

Japanese: 今度こそ‥‥仮面マスクの 最後の事件となるでしょう。
English: This will surely be Mask☆DeMasque's final heist!

Japanese: 前回の事件で、我はヤツから エモノを取り戻すことに成功した！
English: Last time I came close and managed to retrieve the stolen treasure!

Japanese: 明日の裁判で、 ヤツが有罪になったとき！
English: Mark my words, this criminal of humanity will be found guilty in due time!

Japanese: 我が名声は、宙を舞うでしょう。
English: And then I, Luke Atmey, Ace Detective, will dance with joy!

Japanese: ‥‥イミはわかりませんが、 楽しみにしておきます。
English: I have no clue what you're blabbering about... But I guess I'll find out soon.

Japanese: 哀牙さん。 これなんですけど‥‥
English: What about this person here, Detective Atmey?

Japanese: ？　どうしたんですか？ ダマりこんじゃって。
English: Is something wrong? Did the cat get your tongue or something?

Japanese: そのようなものを見せられても、 ムズムズしませんな。
English: That photo leaves me with a distinctly indistinct feeling.

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Ummm...

Japanese: 我がスイリがヘソを 曲げてしまったようです。
English: In this case, I'm afraid my perspicacity has been left wanting.

Japanese: （特に情報はない、って コトかな‥‥）
English: (I think he's trying to say that he doesn't have any information...)

Japanese: ‥‥ザンネンながら‥‥
English: ...I'm sorry I can't be more helpful.

Japanese: 我が好敵手たるライヴァルと 証拠について語るシュミはなし！
English: But I have no interest save that of finding evidence against my arch-rival!

Japanese: いえいえ。なるほどくんなんて、 ケチでしがない弁護士ですから。
English: Nah, it's OK. Nick is just a bit of a piddling evidence hog. That's his defense style.

Japanese: ケチでしがないヤカラに、 我がスイリを語るイミなし！
English: A "piddling evidence hog", you say? In that case, I'd simply be wasting my breath!

Japanese: ‥‥だってさ。
English: Yeah, I guess you're right.

Japanese: “ケチでしがない”よりは “ライバル”のほうがマシだな。
English: Thanks, Maya. Why couldn't you have said I was a "rival", and not a "piddling evidence hog"?

Japanese: おお。チャアミングなる 警備担当者・キリオ嬢ですな。
English: Ah, the charming young lady responsible for security, Ms. Adrian Andrews.

Japanese: あれ。知ってるんですか？
English: Huh? You know her?

Japanese: まあ‥‥いわば、 今回の依頼人、ですからな。
English: Yes, well I should... She was the person who hired me for this job.

Japanese: 我が名探偵・星威岳　哀牙に 警備をまかせた、かしこき女性。
English: A very intelligent woman. After all, she hired Detective Luke Atmey, did she not?

Japanese: （まかせて正解だったのか、 シッパイだったのか‥‥？）
English: (...I'm not entirely convinced that was an intelligent move on her part.)

Japanese: 安心されたい。 ツボはかならず、取り戻します。
English: Do not fear, young lady. I promise I shall retrieve your urn!

Japanese: おねがいします、探偵さん！
English: Thank you, Detective Atmey! I'm counting on you!

Japanese: 名探偵・哀牙と怪人☆仮面マスク ‥‥最後の死闘が、今まさにッ！
English: The final round between Detective Luke Atmey and Mask☆DeMasque is set to begin.

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、ウサンくさい ヒトだよな‥‥）
English: Still, there's something funny about this guy...

Japanese: でもさ。アイガさん、怪人のエモノ を取り戻したんだよ、前回は。
English: Yeah, but don't forget he managed to retrieve the treasure from the last heist.

Japanese: 我が華麗なカツヤクをおさめた ビデオもありますぞ。
English: Yes, I even have a video that details my role in the whole affair.

Japanese: テレビニュウスをキレイに録画 しました。‥‥１本、いかがかな？
English: I recorded it directly from the news on TV. Can I interest you in a copy?

Japanese: ‥‥いいです。
English: ...No thanks.

Japanese: 怪人の予告状‥‥エンブレムが プリントされているんですよね。
English: Mask☆DeMasque's calling card... It's got his emblem printed on it, right?

Japanese: ‥‥ウム‥‥！　さすがは、 我が好敵手にしてライヴァル！
English: ...Quite impressive! But I would expect nothing less from my arch-rival!

Japanese: そのようなゴクヒ情報を キャッチしているとは‥‥
English: I'm surprised to see you in possession of such a top- secret piece of information.

Japanese: まあ。名弁護士と名霊媒師と 名タマゴの名イトコですから。
English: Well, we've got an Ace Lawyer, an Ace Spirit Medium and an Ace Cousin-Apprentice here.

Japanese: この哀牙‥‥どうやら、 貴方がたを気に入りそうですぞ！
English: Yes, indeed... I think I could learn to like you, my fellow ace professionals!

Japanese: （なんかいいコトあるのか？ ‥‥それって）
English: (I don't know if I really want this guy to like us... ever.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 哀牙探偵事務所
English: October 12 Atmey Detective Agency

Japanese: ここが探偵事務所か。 ‥‥アヤシイね、なんか。
English: This is a detective agency? Looks more like the set of some B-grade horror movie.

Japanese: すみませーん。
English: H-Hello? Is anyone here...?

Japanese: ルス‥‥みたいだね。
English: It looks like nobody's home.

Japanese: ちぇ。つまんないの。
English: Hmph. What a waste of time.

Japanese: おみやげに、あそこに 飾ってある額ブチ、もらってく？
English: Hey, I know! Let's take that framed picture back with us as a memento!

Japanese: や、やめておけよ。
English: Don't you dare, Maya!

Japanese: （あとで、また来てみるか‥‥）
English: (Guess we'll have to try back later...)

Japanese: 机には、化学実験用の器具が ズラリと並んでいる。
English: This desk is literally covered with all sorts of chemistry equipment.

Japanese: あたし、ジッケン 大好きなんだよねー。
English: Oh, I just love chemistry!

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん、 知ってた？
English: Say, Nick, I bet you didn't know this, but...

Japanese: お水って、炭素と水素で できているんだよ。
English: Water is made up of carbon and hydrogen!

Japanese: いやいやいや。 炭素じゃなくて、酸素だろ。
English: No, no, no. Not carbon! Oxygen! Oxygen and hydrogen!

Japanese: へええ。 そういう説もあるんだねー。
English: Oh? Well, there are always alternative theories, I guess.

Japanese: （“説”とか、そういう モンダイじゃないと思うけど）
English: (If by "alternative theories" you mean the ones you make up inside that head of yours.)

Japanese: あ。大きなカマドがあるよ！
English: Whoa! It's a huge hearth!

Japanese: それはダンロだよ。
English: That's a fireplace.

Japanese: どうちがうの？ 同じようなものじゃない。
English: So? What's the difference?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ようよ、 なるほどくん。
English: You need to stop judging things based on narrow-minded cultural assumptions, Nick!

Japanese: ははは。
English: R-Right... sorry.

Japanese: リッパな本棚に、リッパな本が ズラリと並んでいる。
English: That's one impressive bookshelf filled with some very impressive books!

Japanese: 《名探偵・星威岳　哀牙全集》 星威岳　哀牙・著。
English: "My Life as an Ace Detective: Unabridged Edition" by Luke Atmey, Ace Detective.

Japanese: 大きなボトルシップも かざってあるな。
English: Hey, he's even got one of those ships in a bottle thingies.

Japanese: あ！　このおフネ、運転席で アイガ探偵の人形が手をふってる！
English: Ah! Would you look at that! There's a little Luke Atmey doll at the helm! How cute!

Japanese: ケンビ鏡もあるぞ。 何か研究しているみたいだ。
English: There's a microscope here, too. I wonder if he was researching something.

Japanese: スライドガラスに標本の 名前が書いてある。
English: The glass slides have different names on them.

Japanese: 《ホシイダケ菌》‥‥だって。
English: This one says... "Atmey Virus". ...Hmm.

Japanese: ‥‥とことん 目立ちたがり屋だねえ‥‥。
English: This guy is just dying to make a name for himself, huh.

Japanese: なんか、ちょっとだけ ハラ立つな。
English: It's getting kind of irritating, actually.

Japanese: 名探偵・星威岳　哀牙が こっちをニラみつけている。
English: What a creepy painting. It's like he's looking right at me.

Japanese: ウチの事務所もさ。 なるほどくんの絵をかざろうよ！
English: Maybe we should put up a big portrait of you in the office, Nick!

Japanese: あたし、かいてあげるから。 トイレ掃除してるところとか。
English: I can even paint it for you. Maybe you can pose while cleaning the toilet?

Japanese: ‥‥できれば フツーの絵にしてほしいな。
English: ...I think I'd prefer something a bit more dignified.

Japanese: なるほどくん。 なあに？　このキカイ。
English: Hey, Nick. What's this thing here?

Japanese: “チクオン機”っていうんだ。
English: It's called a "gramophone".

Japanese: ムカシはこいつで音楽を 聞いたらしいよ。
English: In the old days, people used them to listen to music.

Japanese: なんだか、ウソっぽいなあ。
English: You can't be serious!

Japanese: あたしはね。部屋の空気を キレイにするキカイだと思うな。
English: I think it's probably for purifying the air or something.

Japanese: ５０円、かける？
English: I'll bet you 50 cents! Come on, what do you say?

Japanese: ちゃんと払えよ。
English: Forget it. You never pay up when you lose.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 哀牙探偵事務所
English: October 12 Atmey Detective Agency

Japanese: すみませーん。
English: Hello?

Japanese: ‥‥これはこれは、 チャアミングなおじょうさん。
English: ...Well, well. How lovely it is to see you again, my dear.

Japanese: 我が事務所へようこそ！ ごゆるりと、おくつろぎを‥‥。
English: Welcome to my abode! Relax and soak up the atmosphere...

Japanese: あ、あの。ぼくたちは‥‥
English: Err, umm... We're actually kind of...

Japanese: ‥‥シッ！　ダマって！
English: SHHH! SILENCE!

Japanese: クックックックックッ‥‥ どうやら、そういうコトですな。
English: Hee hee hee hee hee... Precisely as I expected.

Japanese: なな、なんですか？
English: Wh-What is?

Japanese: ‥‥ズヴァリ！ 美しきスイリが我にささやく真実。
English: ...Zvarri! The truth has once again been elegantly revealed to me!

Japanese: 貴方がたは、弁護士と‥‥おそらく 霊媒師。‥‥ちがいますかな？
English: A lawyer, and... a spirit medium. ...Am I correct?

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 さっきも聞きましたけど、それ。
English: ...Umm. I think we've already been through this.

Japanese: ハッ！　美しきスイリはズヴァリ！ ‥‥何度聞いても美しいのですよ。
English: Ha! So my estimation was correct! Zvarri! ...How truly elegant.

Japanese: それでは、貴方がたの お話をうかがいましょうかな！
English: Now then, what can I do for you?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥１つ、 ハッキリしたことがあるよ。
English: Well it looks like we got one thing cleared up anyway.

Japanese: え。なに？　アイガさんが、 等身大の名探偵だったってコト？
English: Huh? What? That Atmey is the greatest Ace Detective in the world?

Japanese: 哀牙探偵は‥‥ 犯人の姿を見ていないんだ。
English: No, that he never actually saw the thief.

Japanese: あ‥‥そうか！
English: Oh... that's right!

Japanese: もしかしたら、犯人は 仮面マスクじゃないかも‥‥
English: So the thief may not even be Mask☆DeMasque...

Japanese: あいや、待たれよ！ ‥‥弁護士殿。さすがにそれは！
English: Just one moment, Sir Lawyer! The thief was unquestionably Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: でも、あなたは 見ていないじゃないですか！
English: But you never actually saw him!

Japanese: ‥‥そういうときのために、 防犯キャメラがあるのですよ。
English: Perhaps so, but I installed a security camera for just such a contingency.
Oh...

Japanese: ゆうべ‥‥キャメラは１回、 作動しています。
English: Last night, the camera went off exactly once.

Japanese: これぞ！　コムピュウタがとらえた 犯人の真の姿なのだッ！
English: Behold! This is a photo of the dastardly thief, taken by the security camera!

Japanese: ‥‥こ、これは‥‥やっぱり！
English: ...Th-That's him!

Japanese: か、かめんますく‥‥だね‥‥
English: M-Mask☆DeMasque...!

Japanese: 《防犯カメラの写真》のデータを 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Security Camera Photo added to the Court Record.

Japanese: 犯人は、怪人☆仮面マスク 以外にはあり得ませぬぞ。
English: The thief can be none other than the arch-criminal, Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: ヤツには‥‥犯行を重ねなければ ならない理由があるのですから。
English: After all, he has a very good reason for committing such brazen crimes.

Japanese: な‥‥なんですって！
English: Wh-What are you talking about?

Japanese: きゃっ！　このカマド、 すずしいカゼが流れてくるよっ！
English: Aaah! There's a cold breeze coming from this hearth!

Japanese: クックックックッ‥‥どうやら、 我がトリックにかかったようです。
English: Hee hee hee hee... It seems you've fallen for my little deception.

Japanese: ダンロには‥‥なんと、意外な キカイが仕込まれているのです。
English: You see, this fireplace conceals a rather unexpected device.

Japanese: ‥‥そう！ エアア・コンディショナアがッ！
English: ...Yes! A high-tech air conditioner!

Japanese: はあ‥‥えあこん、ですか。
English: Wh-Why would you put an air conditioner in there?

Japanese: 夏はすずしく、冬はあたたか。 ‥‥これぞ、新世紀のダンロです。
English: In the summer it acts as an air conditioner and in the winter, it's a heater. Clever?

Japanese: それにしても、 でっかい絵だねー‥‥
English: That's quite a large portrait of yourself you have there...

Japanese: 《アイがほしいだけさ》 ‥‥星威岳　哀牙・作。
English: Yes. I wanted it to say to everyone who saw it, "Look at me, Luke Atmey!"

Japanese: モデルがいいので、 スバラシイ作品になりましたよ。
English: With a model so fine, I knew the portrait would also be aesthetically pleasing!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: ...Um, that's nice.

Japanese: きゃっ！　この部屋の音楽、 ここから聞こえていたんだ！
English: Aaah! So the music in this room is coming from here!

Japanese: 《哀牙のエレジイ》 ‥‥作曲・星威岳　哀牙。
English: "Luke Atmey Elegy" composed by Luke Atmey.

Japanese: それは“チクオン機”と 言いましてな。
English: This lovely device is called a "gramophone".

Japanese: いにしえの人びとは、その キカイで音楽を聞いたのですよ。
English: In days of yore, people listened to music on them.

Japanese: へええ。そうだと思いました！
English: Aha! That's just what I thought! I told you so, Nick!

Japanese: さっきも聞きましたけど、 ゆうべは１人で見張りを‥‥？
English: You mentioned before that you were on guard all alone last night.

Japanese: さよう。これは、我が好敵手との、 ５回目にして最大の勝負。
English: Precisely. This is my fifth encounter with my arch-enemy.

Japanese: ‥‥なんぴとたりとも、ジャマを ユルすワケには行かなかった。
English: I refuse to allow anyone to interfere with the rightful pursuit of my prey.

Japanese: たしか、仮面マスクの最初の事件 から、対決してるんですよね？
English: I heard you've been after Mask☆DeMasque since his very first theft?

Japanese: やあれ、さすがはおじょうさん。 よくご存じだ。
English: Yes, my dear. I see you've done your homework.

Japanese: さよう！　ヤツの最初のエモノ こそ、かの宝石《エマノンの涙》！
English: In his very first heist, that vile thief pilfered the famous jewel, the "Tear of Emanon"!

Japanese: その盗難現場たる美術館で、 我らは奇跡の出会いを果たした！
English: I first encountered him in the museum's sacred hall. The crime scene itself!

Japanese: それで‥‥倉院のツボの警備も？
English: So... that's why you were hired as security for the Treasure Exhibit?

Japanese: （霧緒さんが依頼した、って 言ってたっけな‥‥）
English: (I'm pretty sure he said that Adrian Andrews hired him...)

Japanese: 我は高菱屋より機材を借りうけ、 パアフェクトなワナを張ったのだ！
English: Yes, I borrowed some equipment from Lordly Tailor and set the perfect trap.

Japanese: それが、あの防犯カメラ だったワケですか‥‥
English: You must mean the security camera...

Japanese: でも、アイガさん自身も 現場で見張っていたのに‥‥
English: But I thought you were watching the area yourself, too.

Japanese: 仮面マスク、どうやって ツボを盗んだのかな？
English: So how did Mask☆DeMasque manage to steal the urn?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥。モンダイは 怪人の手口、だな。やっぱり。
English: That's the million dollar question... What is DeMasque's modus operandi?

Japanese: あの‥‥哀牙さん。 倉院のツボって、知ってました？
English: Umm... Mr. Atmey? Did you know about the Sacred Urn?

Japanese: 名探偵が関心をそそぐべきは、 ただ１つ！　‥‥仮面マスクのみ。
English: I'm interested in only one thing my dear, and that is Mask☆DeMasque!

Japanese: ツボ‥‥？　ハッ！ この事件には、関係ありませぬな。
English: Sacred Urn...? Pfft! That has nothing to do with this case.

Japanese: でも‥‥仮面マスクのエモノ だったんですよ？
English: But... wasn't that what Mask☆DeMasque stole this time?

Japanese: ‥‥我が名はハンタア。 ツボなど、怪人のエサにすぎぬ。
English: I am a hunter, sir! The urn was nothing more than a lure to catch my prey.

Japanese: 貴方がたは、ハトに投げたマメの カタチを、おぼえていますかな？
English: Do you yourselves remember the shape of the individual peanuts you throw to pigeons?

Japanese: ‥‥なんかハラ立つねー、 なるほどくん。
English: ...I don't think I like this guy's attitude, Nick.

Japanese: とにかく、探偵さんは ツボを知らない、ってコトだね。
English: Well anyway, it looks like he doesn't know about the urn.

Japanese: あの‥‥ひとつ、気になる コトがあるんですけど。
English: Umm... There's something that's kind of bothering me.

Japanese: 人生とは、ただ１つのコタエを 探し求め、さまようがごとし。
English: Please, my dear, ask me anything you like.

Japanese: ‥‥いったい、なんでしょうかな？
English: After all, we are all but seekers, wandering alone in the dark.

Japanese: 仮面マスク、どうやって ツボを盗んだのかなあ、って。
English: Well, I was wondering how Mask☆DeMasque managed to steal the urn.

Japanese: ‥‥それって、哀牙さんが 知ってなければおかしいですよね。
English: I mean, isn't it strange that you don't seem to know?

Japanese: そりゃそうですね。
English: Yeah, now that you mention it, it is strange.

Japanese: だって、あなた‥‥ 現場を見張っていたんですからね。
English: After all, you were on guard that night at the scene of the crime.

Japanese: イネムリでもしていないかぎり、 仮面マスクと会ったはずでしょう？
English: Unless you were sound asleep, you should have at least seen Mask☆DeMasque.

Japanese: こ‥‥これは‥‥
English: Wh-What the!?

Japanese: サイコ・ロック‥‥！
English: It's a Psyche-Lock...

Japanese: さ‥‥さいこ・ろっく‥‥？
English: Ps-Psyche Lock...?

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)

Japanese: ね。ね。なるほどくん。 なあに、サイコ・ロックって。
English: Hey, Nick, what is this Psyche-Lock thing?

Japanese: この勾玉の“霊力”でね。ココロを 閉ざす“ヒミツ”が見えるんだ。
English: Well, your Magatama lets me see when people are keeping secrets.

Japanese: ロックを解除すれば、そのヒミツを 聞き出すことだってできる。
English: By breaking their mental "locks", I can find out what those secrets are.

Japanese: ふええええ‥‥その勾玉に、 そんなフシギなパワーがッ！
English: Whaaaaat!? This Magatama has THAT kind of power!?

Japanese: あのさ。この勾玉をくれたの、 真宵ちゃんじゃないか。去年。
English: Maya, you're the one who gave me this Magatama last year...

Japanese: ‥‥ま。たしかにそれ、 綾里本家に伝わるモノだけどね。
English: ...Well, it's true that this Magatama is a prized Fey family heirloom, but...

Japanese: ジッサイに霊力をこめたのは はみちゃんだったでしょ？
